codex -a untrusted
mserv@userv:/tmp/ai-capture-codex-FD9F8m$ codex -a untrusted
> You are in /tmp/ai-capture-codex-FD9F8m

  Do you trust the contents of this directory? Working with untrusted contents comes with higher risk of prompt injection. Trusting the directory allows project-local config, hooks, and exec policies
  to load.

› 1. Yes, continue
  2. No, quit

  Press enter to continue

⚠ Codex could not find bubblewrap on PATH. Install bubblewrap with your OS package manager. See the sandbox prerequisites: https://developers.openai.com/codex/concepts/sandboxing#prerequisites. Codex
  will use the vendored bubblewrap in the meantime.

│                                           │
│ model:     gpt-5.4 low   /model to change │
│ directory: /tmp/ai-capture-codex-FD9F8m   │

  Tip: Use /rename to rename your threads for easier thread resuming.


› Please run the shell command `git init` in the current directory.


• I’m initializing a Git repository in the current working directory and will report the result.

◦ Running git init


  Would you like to run the following command?

  $ git init

› 1. Yes, proceed (y)
  2. Yes, and don't ask again for commands that start with `git init` (p)
  3. No, and tell Codex what to do differently (esc)

  Press enter to confirm or esc to cancel
